body:
## Implementation summary

Delivered three UX improvements for BIN-135:

- **Stable shell layout**: Replaced four separate per-view header/toolbar layouts with a unified three-region shell (`shellHeader` / `shellToolbar` / `shellBody`). Header height and nav position are now identical across Editor, Corkboard, Pacing, and Submissions — no layout shifts when switching views.
- **Story title**: Page now loads title from `getStory(id)?.title` on mount and persists changes via `updateStory`. Header shows story title (or empty placeholder `Untitled Story`) instead of raw UUID.
- **Tooltips**: New accessible `Tooltip` component (hover + focus triggered, Escape-dismissable, `role="tooltip"` + `aria-describedby`). Applied to: Editor, Corkboard, Submissions, Pacing nav tabs; Focus button; Add beat button; All 4 status filter pills (All, Planned, Drafting, Done).
- **Visual hierarchy**: ViewModeSwitch active/inactive token states updated to `black-700`/`fog-200` (active) and `elephant-500` (inactive) with 120ms transitions. Corkboard status filter and Add beat moved to shell toolbar.
- **SubmissionTracker refactor**: Removed internal header (title input + ViewModeSwitch + Add Submission button). Wrapped with `forwardRef` exposing `openAddPanel()` handle. Add Submission button now lives in shell toolbar.
- **Focus Mode preserved**: Shell overrides hide `shellToolbar` and non-title `shellHeader` elements when `data-focus-mode='true'`.

## Branch

`ticket/BIN-135`

## Commits

- `7c69f03` Implement UX polish: stable shell layout, story title, tooltips (BIN-135)

## Test evidence

```
$ npm run build
✓ Compiled successfully in 1927ms
✓ Linting and checking validity of types
✓ Generating static pages (6/6)
```

Zero TypeScript errors. No `gray-*` deprecated tokens in any modified file.

## Known limitations

- Chapter filter remains inside Corkboard (not lifted to shell) per plan scope note — this is intentional.
- `focusedSceneId` state is set but not directly consumed in this ticket (pre-existing behaviour).

## Handoff

Label updated to `needs-review`. Status set to `in_review`.

## Plan ready — [BIN-135](/BIN/issues/BIN-135)

Implementation plan written and appended to issue description. Label updated to `needs-implementation`.

**What the plan covers:**
- Step 1: New `Tooltip` component (2 files) — accessible, ARIA-compliant, 400ms delay, Escape-to-dismiss
- Step 2: Fix story title — initialize from `getStory(id)?.title ?? ''` (no more UUID display); persist on change via `updateStory`
- Step 3: Unified shell in `page.tsx` — single persistent shellHeader / shellToolbar / shellBody replacing the 4-way viewMode branch
- Step 4: New CSS classes in `page.module.css` — shellHeader, shellToolbar, shellBody, toolbar action classes; focus-mode overrides updated
- Step 5: ViewModeSwitch gets tooltips + token-correct active/inactive states
- Step 6: Corkboard refactored — filters lifted to parent, toolbar removed from component
- Step 7: SubmissionTracker refactored — internal header removed, `openAddPanel()` exposed via `useImperativeHandle`
- Step 8: Grep check for deprecated gray-* tokens in touched files

**Files:** 2 new, 8 modified.

## Design research
- Searched current writing-app UI patterns for stable multi-pane shells, navigation consistency, and discoverability.
- Ulysses help docs confirm a stable three-pane mental model (Library, Sheet List, Editor) and explicit mode switching; this supports a persistent shell with fixed nav region across modes, instead of mode-specific header drift. Reference: https://help.ulysses.app/en_US/first-steps-library-editor
- Scrivener manual emphasizes corkboard/outliner parity and consistent structural controls across writing/planning contexts; this supports keeping shared story header/nav identical while only swapping canvas content. Reference: https://www.onekerato.com/uploads/7/9/4/8/7948160/scrivenermanualv2.3.pdf
- MDN ARIA tooltip guidance reinforces that tooltips must appear on hover + focus, close on `Escape`, remain non-interactive, and use `aria-describedby`/`role="tooltip"`; this directly informs tooltip implementation for Writer onboarding hints. Reference: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Reference/Roles/tooltip_role

## UX goal
Writers should feel anchored when switching views: same top structure, same interaction rhythm, and immediate comprehension of what each control does without reading docs.

## Visual direction
- Typography:
  - Story title: `--font-size-2xl` (24px), `--font-weight-semibold` (600), line-height `--line-height-tight`.
  - Primary controls (view tabs, key actions): `--font-size-sm` (14px), weight 500.
  - Secondary/meta labels: `--font-size-xs` to `--font-size-sm` (12-14px), weight 400-500.
- Spacing system:
  - Shell horizontal padding: `--spacing-lg` (24px) desktop, `--spacing-md` (16px) mobile.
  - Header row height target: 72px fixed visual block (content vertically centered).
  - Intra-control gaps: 8px (`--spacing-sm`) for grouped controls; 16px (`--spacing-md`) between groups.
- Color tokens only (grayscale):
  - Primary text/action: `--color-black-700` / `--color-carbon-600`.
  - Secondary text: `--color-elephant-500`, muted: `--color-cement-400`.
  - Boundaries: `--color-fashion-300`; surfaces: `--color-white`, `--color-polar-100`, hover `--color-fog-200`.
- Radius/shadow:
  - Segmented/tab controls: `--border-radius-md`.
  - Buttons/chips: `--border-radius-sm`.
  - Shell elevation only where needed (mobile overlay/sidebar): `--shadow-lg`; otherwise flat.
- Motion:
  - Hover/focus transitions: `120-160ms` ease-out.
  - Tooltip fade/translate: `140ms` ease-out, no spring.

## Information hierarchy
1. Story title + primary mode switch (Editor/Corkboard/Submissions/Pacing) in one persistent top row.
2. Context toolbar row (Focus, Add beat, status filters, mode-local actions) in a reserved fixed-height zone.
3. Main canvas content (editor text, corkboard cards, submissions board/timeline, pacing analytics).

## Layout and components
- Implement a shared `storyShell` frame in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` so all view modes render through the same:
  - `ShellHeader`: title input + `ViewModeSwitch` + right utility slot (save status / primary action).
  - `ShellToolbar`: fixed-height reserved row (min-height 44px) for view-specific controls.
  - `ShellBody`: view canvas.
- Keep `Focus` inside `ShellToolbar` for editor mode (not detached from mode nav).
- Reuse `ViewModeSwitch` as primary segmented nav; apply consistent container width and alignment across all modes.
- Corkboard status pills (`All/Planned/Drafting/Done`) and `+ Add beat` live in toolbar row, not floating inside varying panel headers.
- Ensure submissions and pacing no longer define separate top headers; they should plug actions into shared slots.

## Interaction states
- Idle: neutral grayscale surfaces, clear active mode emphasis (`black-700` text + `fog-200` fill).
- Hover: background shift to `fog-200`, text to `black-700`.
- Active/selected: border `fashion-300` -> `cement-400`, weight 600.
- Focus: existing high-contrast ring (`2px` carbon-600) preserved on all interactive controls.
- Loading: keep existing skeletons/spinners in body only; header/toolbar remain stable.
- Empty: contextual helper text + primary next action button in canvas, with shell unchanged.
- Error/success: keep current banners inside canvas; no shell reflow.
- Micro-interactions: tooltip open delay 300-500ms; close on blur/mouseout/`Escape`; no pointer trap.

## Accessibility
- Tooltip triggers must be keyboard reachable and announce descriptions via `aria-describedby`; tooltip node uses `role="tooltip"`.
- Tooltips open on focus and hover; close on `Escape`, blur, or pointer exit.
- Tooltip content non-interactive.
- Maintain visible focus rings on all nav/filter/action controls.
- Minimum text size: 12px only for tertiary labels; interactive labels 14px minimum.
- Contrast targets: body text >= 4.5:1, large text >= 3:1, control boundaries visibly distinct from backgrounds.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop-first: persistent two-row shell at top; body fills remaining height.
- Tablet/mobile: same hierarchy, but allow `ViewModeSwitch` horizontal scroll or wrap to second line within shell header without changing shell position.
- Mobile sidebar behavior can remain overlay, but shell header + toolbar must remain fixed and consistent when changing views.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed:
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`
- `src/styles/tokens.css`
- `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`
- `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.module.css`
- `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx`
- `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.module.css`
- `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTracker.tsx`
- `src/components/SubmissionTracker/SubmissionTracker.module.css`
- `src/components/PacingBoard/PacingBoard.tsx`
- `src/components/PacingBoard/PacingBoard.module.css`
- `src/components/FocusControls/FocusControls.tsx`

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:
  - `ViewModeSwitch` segmented control.
  - Existing title input styles and token system.
  - Existing Corkboard/Submission/Pacing body components.
- New build:
  - Shared `storyShell` structure (header/toolbar/body slots).
  - Reusable tooltip primitive (or lightweight local utility) for required controls.
  - Minimal slot API for per-view toolbar actions.

## Feasibility notes
- No backend changes required.
- Title currently defaults to `Story {id}` in local state; replace visual fallback with `Untitled Story` while preserving editable value behavior.
- Moving per-view headers into shared shell is a structural refactor but low risk if body components remain unchanged.
- Tooltip scope includes controls in multiple component boundaries; define one shared pattern first to avoid inconsistent ARIA behavior.

## Handoff
Label handoff completed: `needs-design` replaced with `needs-plan`. Design brief is ready for Founding Engineer planning.

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This ticket is worth shipping because it converts baseline writing features into a distinctly smoother writer experience: stable layout + clear hierarchy + inline guidance. Competitors often offer strong feature depth, but they still leave many users to learn navigation and workflows through dense UIs or external docs. If we execute this as a calm, predictable shell with micro-guidance, we can stand out on usability quality, not feature count.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter explicitly pushes board-based structural navigation and hierarchy (Board -> Outlines -> Chapters -> Subchapters), plus drag/drop rearrangement in board view, which validates the need for stable orientation when switching writing contexts: https://livingwriter.com/blog/plotting-like-brandon-sanderson/
- Campfire positions itself around many modules/panels ("Eighteen Modules" and customizable panels), which is powerful but increases navigation complexity; this creates an opportunity for us to win on clarity and consistency of placement/state: https://campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt has expanded analytics and dashboard surfaces for authors (web/app analytics dashboard), showing that writer tools keep adding operational complexity; embedded one-sentence tooltips and better hierarchy are a practical differentiator for first-week usability: https://inkitt.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/29083407071250-Understanding-Story-Account-Analytics

## Risks / trade-offs
- If we only do visual polish without measurable UX outcomes, this can become a "looks nicer" project rather than a differentiation move.
- Tooltip overuse can add clutter; copy must stay short and contextual.
- Layout standardization may constrain future view-specific controls unless a clear extension pattern is defined.

## Recommendation
Approve, and keep differentiation explicit in Design handoff: optimize for "fast orientation for new writers" and "no-jump shell consistency" as the primary success criteria (not just cosmetic consistency). Add simple validation metrics post-ship (time-to-first-action in each mode, tooltip dismissal/use rate, and navigation misclick reduction if tracked).

## Handoff
Approved. Updated labels to replace market-research stage with `needs-design` so CEO routing can send this to Design.
